curriculum.title: Advertising Unplugged: 90-Day Growth Challenge
curriculum.description: A structured 12-week journey to transform marketing from overwhelming to actionable for entrepreneurs, founders, and SMEs.
curriculum.philosophy: Clarity Over Noise. Purpose Beyond Profit.

weeks.description: Establish a clear baseline of your current brand position, marketing effectiveness, and competitive standing. This becomes your benchmark for measuring 90-day progress.
objectives: Conduct a comprehensive brand audit across all touchpoints
Identify marketing gaps and strengths in your current efforts
Define what success looks like for your business over 90 days
Create a baseline for measuring growth and ROI
weeks.keyConceptSummary: Your brand isn't what you say it is—it's what your customers experience. A brand audit examines every customer touchpoint to understand what's working, what's broken, and what's missing.

tasks.title: Complete the Marketing Audit Template
tasks.description: Score your current performance across Website/SEO, Social Media, Email, Content, Paid Ads, Brand Consistency, Customer Experience, Analytics Tracking

tasks.title: Document your current customer journey
tasks.description: Map where customers first hear about you, evaluate you, buy, and stay in touch. Note pain points.

tasks.title: Audit your website
tasks.description: Check page load speed, mobile responsiveness, CTA clarity, trust signals, navigation ease. Document 5 improvements needed.

tasks.title: Review last 3 months of marketing data
tasks.description: Gather metrics from Google Analytics, social media insights, email platform, paid ads. Identify which channels drove traffic and conversions.

tasks.title: Analyze top 3 competitors
tasks.description: Visit websites, follow social, sign up for email. Note messaging, content strategy, social presence, customer reviews.

tasks.title: Interview 3 recent customers
tasks.description: Ask how they found you, their decision process, what almost stopped them, what makes you worth recommending.

tasks.title: Define 90-day success metrics
tasks.description: Choose 3-5 KPIs to track weekly: Website traffic, email list growth, leads, conversion rate, CAC, brand awareness.

weeks.milestone: Completed Marketing Audit document with baseline metrics, competitive insights, and prioritized list of top 10 marketing improvements
reflectionPrompt: What surprised you most about your current marketing efforts? What are you doing well that you underestimate?
If you had to describe your current marketing in one sentence, is that aligned with your actual business goals?
weeks.communityTopic: What's the biggest gap you discovered between how you thought your marketing was performing and the actual baseline data?

bonusResources.title: Google Analytics Academy
bonusResources.description: Free certification to master analytics platform

weeks.description: Build detailed customer personas that move beyond demographics to psychographics, pain points, and decision-making triggers. Your marketing will be 10x more effective when targeted to a crystal-clear audience.
objectives: Create 2-3 detailed, data-backed customer personas
Understand your customers' biggest pain points and aspirations
Identify where and how your ideal customers spend their attention
Map the decision-making journey from awareness to purchase
weeks.keyConceptSummary: Real personas include Demographics + Psychographics + Behavior + Pain Points + Decision Criteria + Objections. Not 'women 25-45' but 'Sarah is a bootstrapped founder who...'

tasks.title: Complete Customer Persona Builder
tasks.description: Create 2-3 personas with Demographics, Psychographics, Behavior, Pain Points, Aspirations, Decision Criteria, Objections

tasks.title: Conduct 5 customer interviews
tasks.description: Ask: How you found us, buying process, what almost stopped you, what makes you recommend us, what you wish we offered

tasks.title: Survey your customer base
tasks.description: 5-question form: How you heard about us, biggest challenge, what matters in purchasing, what to change

tasks.title: Research where customers hang out online
tasks.description: Identify 3-5 platforms (Reddit, LinkedIn groups, Facebook groups, forums, podcasts, newsletters) and document discussions

tasks.title: Document pain point language
tasks.description: Highlight exact phrases customers use to describe problems. These become your marketing words.

tasks.title: Create competitive persona
tasks.description: Build persona for top competitor's ideal customer. How different from yours? Where is overlap?

tasks.title: Map customer decision journey
tasks.description: For each persona, timeline: What triggers need awareness, where they search, info gathered, decision influencers, final push to buy

weeks.milestone: 2-3 detailed customer personas with supporting research, decision journey maps, and list of top pain point phrases from customer research
reflectionPrompt: When you read your finished persona, what surprises you? What assumption did your research contradict?
Which persona excites you most to serve? Which is most profitable?
weeks.communityTopic: Share one unexpected insight from your customer research. What did you learn about your ideal customer that you didn't expect?

bonusResources.title: Typeform or Google Forms
bonusResources.description: Free survey tools for customer research

bonusResources.title: Superhuman
bonusResources.description: Email tool showing intent data about prospect engagement

weeks.description: Crystallize your unique value proposition and create a messaging framework that differentiates you from competitors and resonates with your ideal customer.
objectives: Define your unique positioning in the marketplace
Craft a compelling value proposition (not a tagline—a clear statement of differentiation)
Create a messaging hierarchy: brand essence, key messages, supporting proof points
Develop message variations for different audience segments
weeks.keyConceptTitle: Positioning Is a Promise You Keep
weeks.keyConceptSummary: Positioning is a strategic choice about where you compete and how you differentiate. Internal clarity: 'For X audience, with Y problem, we're better than Z alternative because A, B, C'

tasks.title: Complete Brand Positioning Pyramid
tasks.description: Define Brand Purpose, Promise, Values (3-4), and Personality

tasks.title: Write your positioning statement
tasks.description: Use framework: 'For [audience] who [problem], [Brand] is [category] that [value] unlike [alternative] which [drawback]'

tasks.title: Identify your points of difference
tasks.description: Using competitive analysis, list unique differentiators (Quality, Speed, Price, Service, Innovation, Expertise, Methodology). Pick 2-3 with evidence.

tasks.title: Create your value proposition
tasks.description: Single sentence: 'We help [audience] [achieve outcome] by [unique method]'. Test on 2-3 customers.

tasks.title: Build messaging matrix
tasks.description: 4-5 key messages with Target Audience, Proof Point, Example, Tone for each

tasks.title: Develop message variations
tasks.description: How messaging differs by channel/persona (Founder vs Manager, Email vs LinkedIn). What stays consistent? What changes?

tasks.title: Audit current messaging
tasks.description: Review homepage, email templates, social bios, sales calls. How much aligns with new positioning? What needs to change?

weeks.milestone: Completed Brand Positioning document with positioning statement, points of difference, value proposition, messaging matrix, and one-page brand messaging guide
reflectionPrompt: Does your positioning feel authentic? Could you defend it to a skeptical competitor?
What message, once you say it aloud, immediately feels true and energizing?
weeks.communityTopic: What's your positioning statement? Run it by the community and see if it's clear, compelling, and credible.

bonusResources.title: Grammarly Premium
bonusResources.description: Ensure all messaging is clear and on-brand

weeks.description: Build a deep competitive intelligence system that informs your strategy, positions you distinctly, and reveals market opportunities.
objectives: Conduct thorough competitive analysis across direct and indirect competitors
Identify market gaps and positioning opportunities
Understand competitor messaging, channels, and customer acquisition strategies
Define your sustainable competitive advantages
weeks.keyConceptTitle: Competitive Analysis Is Strategic Intelligence, Not Paranoia
weeks.keyConceptSummary: Three competitor types: Direct (same solution, same audience), Indirect (different solution, same problem), Adjacent (similar audience, different solution). Analysis reveals gaps and strategic opportunities.

tasks.title: Complete Competitor Analysis Framework
tasks.description: Profile 3-5 direct and 2-3 indirect competitors: Value Proposition, Target Audience, Messaging, Pricing, Features, Channels, Reviews, Strengths, Weaknesses

tasks.title: Deep-dive competitor marketing
tasks.description: Review last 20 social posts, read 5-10 reviews, analyze pricing page, download lead magnet, audit website structure

tasks.title: Monitor competitor pricing and positioning
tasks.description: Create competitive pricing matrix. What do they charge? What's included? How positioned? Where's white space?

tasks.title: Analyze competitor keywords and SEO
tasks.description: Use Ubersuggest or AnswerThePublic to see competitor keywords, their ranking topics, their SEO dominance areas

tasks.title: Identify competitive advantages
tasks.description: Document: What we do better/faster/cheaper? What do we understand about customers competitors miss? What's our sustainable advantage?

tasks.title: Find market gaps
tasks.description: What are customers asking for that competitors don't address? What problems underserved? What segments ignored? Find 3-5 opportunities.

tasks.title: Create competitive positioning map
tasks.description: 2x2 grid with two important axes (Price vs Ease, Speed vs Comprehensiveness). Plot competitors and yourself. Defensible position?

weeks.milestone: Completed competitive analysis with profiles of 5-8 competitors, positioning map, identified market gaps, and summary of sustainable competitive advantages
reflectionPrompt: What competitor activity surprised or concerned you? What should you watch closely?
Where is the biggest opportunity to differentiate based on what competitors ignore?
weeks.communityTopic: What competitive advantage are you most confident defending? How would you explain it in a 30-second pitch?

bonusResources.title: Semrush or Ahrefs
bonusResources.description: Comprehensive competitor SEO and ad analysis—free trials

bonusResources.title: G2 or Capterra
bonusResources.description: Read detailed customer reviews to understand pain points

bonusResources.title: SparkLoop or Feedly
bonusResources.description: Monitor competitor content and social activity

weeks.description: Define your growth targets, allocate budget by channel, and set clear quarterly objectives that ensure every marketing activity connects to business outcomes.
objectives: Set SMART growth targets for 90 days (aligned with business objectives)
Create a marketing budget that balances brand building with lead generation
Define OKRs (Objectives and Key Results) that guide all marketing decisions
Establish a budget allocation framework by channel (organic vs paid, awareness vs conversion)
weeks.keyConceptTitle: Budget Allocation Is Strategy Made Real
weeks.keyConceptSummary: Strategic budgeting: 60% maintenance, 25% growth/optimization, 15% innovation. Within growth: 30% awareness, 50% leads, 20% retention. OKRs force priorities.

tasks.title: Define 90-day business objectives
tasks.description: Start business top-down: What must the business achieve? Revenue? Customers? Milestones? What's marketing's role?

tasks.title: Complete Budget Planner
tasks.description: Document total budget (cash/time), current spend by channel, proposed allocation by channel, estimated ROI per channel

tasks.title: Set 90-day growth targets
tasks.description: Specific targets: Website traffic, email list, leads, customers, CAC, conversion rates, brand awareness. Stretch but achievable.

tasks.title: Create marketing OKRs
tasks.description: Write 2-3 Objectives (aspirational) with 2-3 Key Results each (measurable outcomes) that ladder to business goals

tasks.title: Allocate budget by channel
tasks.description: Based on competitive analysis and customer research, decide allocation: SEO? Paid social? Email? Partnerships? Community? Define percentages and dollars.

tasks.title: Create 12-week initiative calendar with budget
tasks.description: Plan specific initiatives (campaigns, content pillars, email, partnerships) with estimated spend and expected return by week

tasks.title: Define success metrics dashboard
tasks.description: Choose 5-7 weekly metrics (ladder to OKRs). Build tracking sheet (spreadsheet or Metabase) for actuals vs targets

weeks.milestone: Marketing strategy document with 90-day business objectives, marketing OKRs, budget allocation by channel, 12-week initiative calendar with spend, and success metrics dashboard
reflectionPrompt: Does your budget allocation feel realistic? What assumptions need validation?
If you cut budget in half, what would you cut? That reveals what matters most.
weeks.communityTopic: Share your primary marketing OKR for 90 days. What will success look like, and how will you know if you've achieved it?

bonusResources.title: Lattice or 15Five
bonusResources.description: Free tier for managing OKRs with team

bonusResources.title: Marketing benchmarks by industry
bonusResources.description: SiriusDecisions, HubSpot, Neil Patel for CAC/conversion rates

weeks.description: Decide which channels will be your core growth levers for the next 90 days, and build a content plan that fuels those channels with consistent, on-brand content.
objectives: Select 2-3 core marketing channels based on audience and business model
Build a content strategy aligned with buyer journey stages (awareness, consideration, decision)
Create a 12-week content calendar with specific topics, formats, and distribution
Establish a sustainable content production workflow
weeks.keyConceptSummary: Pick 1-2 primary channels where you build audience, 1 owned channel (email, community) for relationships, amplify via others. Focused beats scattered.

tasks.title: Complete Channel Strategy Scorecard
tasks.description: Score each potential channel 0-5: Audience Presence, Business Model Fit, Production Capacity, Competitive Opportunity. Select top 2-3.

tasks.title: Map content to buyer journey
tasks.description: 3x3 grid: Rows = Journey Stage (Awareness, Consideration, Decision), Columns = Format (Blog, Video, Social, Email, Lead Magnet). Brainstorm each cell.

tasks.title: Create content pillar strategy
tasks.description: Define 3-5 core topics your brand owns. Every piece of content ladders to one pillar. Examples: 'DIY Strategy', 'Founder Marketing', 'Metrics', 'Content That Converts'

tasks.title: Build 12-week content calendar
tasks.description: Plan 1-2 pieces per week on primary channels plus email. Include: Topic, Format, Channel, Date, Owner, Status. Realistic and consistent.

tasks.title: Create 2-3 content assets in detail
tasks.description: For top performing ideas, plan actual assets: outline, key points, examples, visuals. Prevents 'I don't know what to write' paralysis.

tasks.title: Define social media strategy
tasks.description: For each channel: Posting cadence, content mix (%), engagement tactics, hashtags, repurposing plan

tasks.title: Plan email strategy
tasks.description: Outline: Welcome sequence, regular cadence, email types (educational, promotional, community), segmentation, content pillars

weeks.milestone: Channel strategy document with scorecard and rationale, content pillars, 12-week content calendar (24-36 pieces), detailed outlines for top 3 assets, email and social strategies
reflectionPrompt: Looking at your 12-week calendar, does this feel sustainable? Where might you get stuck?
What topic could you teach without prep? That's where you start.
weeks.communityTopic: What's your primary content channel and why? What's your first content asset?

bonusResources.title: Buffer or Later
bonusResources.description: Free social media scheduling for consistency

bonusResources.title: Answer the Public or Ubersuggest
bonusResources.description: Discover what questions your audience searches—content topics

weeks.description: Begin content production and establish SEO fundamentals so organic search becomes a long-term customer acquisition channel.
objectives: Create your first 3-4 pillar content pieces
Optimize content for both readers and search engines
Understand SEO basics: keywords, structure, technical SEO
Build a content repurposing system to maximize content ROI
weeks.keyConceptSummary: Paid ads stop when you stop paying. Content works for years. A ranking blog post brings traffic months/years after. Highest ROI with proper execution and SEO optimization.

tasks.title: Conduct keyword research
tasks.description: Use free tool (Google Keyword Planner, Ubersuggest, AnswerThePublic). Find 10-15 keywords audience searches for. Document: Keyword, Volume, Competition, Intent.

tasks.title: Create pillar content piece
tasks.description: Write comprehensive blog/guide (2,000-3,500 words). Structure: Hook, Overview, Section 1-3, Examples, CTA. Optimize for readability.

tasks.title: Optimize for SEO
tasks.description: Include: Target keyword in title/first 100 words, meta description, internal links, external links, alt text for images, mobile check. Use Yoast/Ubersuggest.

tasks.title: Create 2-3 supporting content pieces
tasks.description: Shorter content (800-1,500 words) targeting related keywords, linking to pillar post. Content clusters Google loves.

tasks.title: Plan content repurposing system
tasks.description: For each piece: Twitter thread (3-5), LinkedIn post (carousel), Email, Slack, Short video (15-60 sec), Podcast clip. Document templates.

tasks.title: Set up SEO tracking
tasks.description: Create spreadsheet or use Google Search Console. Track: Posts ranking, keywords, ranking position. Establish baseline.

tasks.title: Audit existing content
tasks.description: Review old posts. Which could be updated? Which already rank? Optimize existing content for better rankings.

weeks.milestone: Published pillar content (2,000+ words, optimized), 2-3 supporting pieces, keyword research document, content repurposing checklist
reflectionPrompt: What surprised you about keyword research? Were people searching for what you thought?
What part of content creation was hardest? What will you do differently next time?
weeks.communityTopic: Share your pillar content topic and primary target keyword. What benefit are you promising readers?

bonusResources.title: Grammarly or Hemingway Editor
bonusResources.description: Free tools to improve writing clarity

weeks.description: Build a strategic email list and create automated email sequences that nurture prospects and drive conversions with minimal ongoing effort.
objectives: Establish email as a core customer acquisition channel
Build a lead magnet and landing page that convert visitors to subscribers
Create automated email sequences (welcome, nurture, sales) that guide prospects
Understand email segmentation and personalization basics
weeks.keyConceptTitle: Email Is Your Most Valuable Channel
weeks.keyConceptSummary: Email is the only touchpoint you truly own. Email ROI is 40x—for every $1 spent, businesses get $40 back. Strategic approach: list growth + nurture sequences + regular communication.

tasks.title: Define your lead magnet
tasks.description: Create specific, valuable asset solving micro-problem: Checklist, audit, script, framework, guide. Document: Topic, Format, Target Audience, Benefit.

tasks.title: Create lead magnet asset
tasks.description: Produce actual asset: Write guide, build template, create video, etc. 30-45 min to go through. Utility > Design.

tasks.title: Build landing page for conversion
tasks.description: Use Webflow, Carrd, or Leadpages. Include: Benefit headline, clear description, form (name+email), CTA. Test for 5-second understanding.

tasks.title: Create welcome email sequence
tasks.description: Plan 3-5 emails: Email 1 (welcome), Email 2 (story), Email 3 (value), Email 4 (offer). Write first 2-3 in full.

tasks.title: Set up email automation
tasks.description: Choose platform (Mailchimp, Brevo, ConvertKit, Substack). Create welcome sequence as automation triggered on signup. Test yourself.

tasks.title: Create lead magnet distribution plan
tasks.description: How will prospects find it? Website, first content, social CTAs, partnerships, ads. Focus on organic distribution Week 8.

tasks.title: Document email strategy
tasks.description: Use Email Playbook: Lead magnet details, landing page, welcome sequence, content pillars, segmentation plan, frequency

weeks.milestone: Live lead magnet asset, functional landing page, automated welcome sequence (3+ emails), documented email strategy and distribution plan
reflectionPrompt: Does your welcome sequence feel authentic to how you communicate with customers?
What's the most common objection your lead magnet should address? Did you address it?
weeks.communityTopic: What's your lead magnet? Would you sign up for it? If not, what needs to change?

bonusResources.title: Mailchimp or Brevo
bonusResources.description: Free email platforms to get started

bonusResources.title: Typeform
bonusResources.description: Build beautiful forms with better conversion

weeks.description: Design and launch your first conversion-focused campaign using both organic and paid channels to accelerate customer acquisition.
objectives: Design a complete marketing campaign from awareness through conversion
Understand paid advertising fundamentals and best practices
Create an ad brief and plan ad copy, targeting, and creative
Measure campaign performance and optimize for ROI
weeks.keyConceptSummary: Campaigns are coordinated activities with single goal and timeline. Phases: Awareness (reach) + Engagement (value) + Conversion (offer) + Retention (loyalty). Paid ads accelerate but content is the hammer.

tasks.title: Define first campaign
tasks.description: Goal, target audience, awareness/engagement/conversion tactics, timeline (2-4 weeks), budget, success metrics. Example: '50 email signups in 4 weeks, $500 budget'

tasks.title: Complete Campaign Dashboard
tasks.description: Document: Name, objective, audience, channels, dates, budget, metrics, tracking URLs, launch checklist

tasks.title: Create ad brief
tasks.description: Goal, target audience, benefit/promise, proof point, CTA, ad variations to test

tasks.title: Design ad creatives
tasks.description: 2-3 variations: Awareness (benefit-focused), Engagement (teaching), Conversion (offer+urgency). Use Canva free or hire freelancer ($50-200)

tasks.title: Set up tracking pixels
tasks.description: Install Facebook/Google pixels on website. Create UTM parameters for campaign URLs. Enable conversion measurement.

tasks.title: Launch campaign
tasks.description: Set up paid ads (Facebook/Instagram or LinkedIn) OR organic push (social, email, partnerships). Start with $20/day for testing.

tasks.title: Create measurement plan
tasks.description: Track: Impressions, clicks, conversions, cost per lead, landing page conversion rate, email growth, sales. Establish week 1 baseline.

weeks.milestone: Live campaign with ads/promotion running, campaign dashboard with tracking, 2-3 ad variations created, initial 1-week performance data
reflectionPrompt: What surprised you about early campaign performance? What's working better/worse than expected?
If you doubled budget, what would you do more of?
weeks.communityTopic: Share your campaign goal and first week results. What surprised you about audience response?

bonusResources.title: Canva
bonusResources.description: Free design tool for creating ads

bonusResources.title: Facebook Ads Manager
bonusResources.description: Free to create and run ads with modest budgets

weeks.description: Establish clear measurement of what's working, calculate true ROI, and begin optimizing your best-performing channels and campaigns.
objectives: Understand key marketing metrics and what they actually mean
Calculate customer acquisition cost (CAC) and lifetime value (LTV) for your business
Set up dashboards for weekly performance tracking
Identify your best-performing channels and campaigns for scaling
weeks.keyConceptSummary: Without measurement, you're guessing. Clarity: Top funnel (awareness), Middle (interest), Bottom (conversion), Unit economics (CAC, LTV, ratio). 3:1 LTV:CAC is sustainable.

tasks.title: Set up measurement dashboard
tasks.description: Google Sheets or Data Studio. Track: Channel, Metric, Weekly targets vs actuals. 5-7 core KPIs. Check every Monday.

tasks.title: Calculate customer acquisition cost (CAC)
tasks.description: Total marketing spend / New paying customers = CAC. Do per-channel if possible. Is this profitable?

tasks.title: Estimate customer lifetime value (LTV)
tasks.description: Average revenue per customer × Average customer lifetime (months) = LTV. Is LTV at least 3x CAC?

tasks.title: Audit your data setup
tasks.description: Ensure: Customer origin tracking, analytics platform proper setup, UTM parameters on campaigns, conversion flow tracking, attribution clarity

tasks.title: Model scenarios with ROI Calculator
tasks.description: If I spend $X on Y channel, how many leads/customers? Expected ROI? Use to decide budget allocation.

tasks.title: Identify top-performing channels
tasks.description: Which have: Lowest cost per lead? Highest conversion? Best ROI? Focus here. Which underperform? Plan to reduce or eliminate.

tasks.title: Analyze campaign performance
tasks.description: Week 9 campaign final numbers: Spend, leads, conversions, cost per lead, conversion rate. What worked? What didn't? What to change next?

weeks.milestone: Live measurement dashboard with 10 weeks data, calculated CAC/LTV, ROI analysis of channels/campaigns, documented optimization opportunities
reflectionPrompt: Does your CAC feel right? Are you spending too much or too little per acquisition?
Which channel is your best performer? What if you doubled investment there?
weeks.communityTopic: What's your customer acquisition cost? Is it sustainable? What would improve it?

weeks.description: Build moats around your brand by establishing community, strategic partnerships, and authority positioning that create defensible competitive advantage.
objectives: Develop a community strategy that turns customers into advocates
Identify and structure strategic partnerships that expand reach
Build positioning as an expert/authority in your space
Create systems for ongoing word-of-mouth and referral marketing
weeks.keyConceptTitle: Community and Authority Are Long-Term Competitive Advantages
weeks.keyConceptSummary: Paid ads reach everyone. Community + authority reach your people and create defensible competitive moats. Founder in your community will recommend before searching.

tasks.title: Define community strategy
tasks.description: Choose format: Online group (Facebook/Slack), In-person events, Mastermind, Membership, or Podcast network. Document: Problem solved, moderation, member value.

tasks.title: Launch community MVP
tasks.description: Create Facebook/Slack/Circle. Seed with best customers. Create 1-2 starter conversations. Or plan first in-person event.

tasks.title: Identify partnership opportunities
tasks.description: List: Complementary services, platforms with your audience, influencers, publications/podcasts. Approach 3-5 with specific proposal.

tasks.title: Plan strategic partnership
tasks.description: With one partner: Co-hosted webinar, guest exchange, affiliate, joint offer. Document: Partner, goal, deliverables, timeline.

tasks.title: Create case study library
tasks.description: Ask 3-5 customers for testimonial/case study. Document: Before situation, transformation, results, lessons. Write 2-3 full case studies.

tasks.title: Apply to speak/get featured
tasks.description: Identify 5 podcasts/publications where audience listens. Pitch as guest with specific insight angle. Aim for 2-3 placements.

tasks.title: Document partnerships and community
tasks.description: Use Partnership Plan template: Partner name, initiative, goal, timeline, owner, status. Create community management plan.

weeks.milestone: Live community launched, 2-3 strategic partnerships executed, 2-3 published case studies, partnership and community management plan documented
reflectionPrompt: Who are your best customers who could become advocates? Have you asked them to help?
What partnership would meaningfully accelerate growth if executed well?
weeks.communityTopic: What's your partnership strategy? What potential partners could you collaborate with?

bonusResources.title: Notchlist
bonusResources.description: Find podcasts to pitch as guest

weeks.weekTitle: Growth Roadmap & Transition to Sustainable Growth
weeks.description: Synthesize 90 days of learning, celebrate progress, and design a sustainable growth roadmap for the next phase so momentum doesn't stop at Week 13.
objectives: Measure progress against 90-day goals and celebrate wins
Analyze what worked, what didn't, and why
Design a sustainable marketing operating model for ongoing execution
Plan the next 90 days with refined strategy and priorities
weeks.keyConceptTitle: Week 12 Is the Beginning, Not the End
weeks.keyConceptSummary: 90 days were a sprint. Now build a marathon. Week 12 creates systems and plans that carry momentum forward. Sustainable model: Clear ownership, realistic cadence, evergreen systems, monthly reviews.

tasks.title: Calculate 90-day results
tasks.description: Week 1 baseline vs Week 12 actual across all success metrics. Quantify: Customers, subscribers, traffic growth, revenue impact. Document wins.

tasks.title: Conduct 90-day retrospective
tasks.description: By phase: What worked? Why? What didn't? Do less of? Identify 3-5 core learnings. Examples: 'Content > Paid Ads', 'LinkedIn works, TikTok doesn't'

tasks.title: Score your brand health
tasks.description: Use Brand Health Scorecard: Awareness, preference, NPS/satisfaction, trust. Self-assess to show direction.

tasks.title: Create NPS survey
tasks.description: Send to customers: 'On scale 0-10, recommend us? Why?' Responses show loyalty. Aim for 40+ NPS score.

tasks.title: Design sustainable operating model
tasks.description: Document: Ownership, weekly cadence (content Tuesday, email Wednesday, etc), tools, monthly review process

tasks.title: Plan next 90 days
tasks.description: Which channels double down? Which stop? New tactics to test? Refine Q2 OKRs. Set new growth targets.

tasks.title: Create 12-month growth roadmap
tasks.description: Q1 milestones, Q2 initiatives, Q3 scaling, Q4 authority. This is your marketing GPS for next year.

weeks.milestone: 90-day results summary, comprehensive retrospective with learnings, sustainable operating model, NPS results, and clear 90-day roadmap for next quarter
reflectionPrompt: What's the biggest progress made in 90 days? What does that success make possible?
If you could go back to Week 1 with what you know now, what would you do differently?
weeks.communityTopic: Celebrate your 90-day wins! What metric are you most proud of? What's your biggest learning?

templateReference.marketingAudit: Week 1: Baseline audit across all channels
templateReference.okrTemplate: Week 5: Growth objectives and key results

principles: Clarity Over Noise — every activity produces clarity
Purpose Beyond Profit — marketing aligned to business outcomes
Actionable, Not Theoretical — tangible deliverables each week
Build on Progress — each week builds on previous learning
Sustainable Systems — by Week 12, systems run without heroic effort
Community as Asset — growth easier and more fun together

graduationPaths.title: AI Strategy Builder
graduationPaths.description: Generate full annual strategy from refined 90-day learnings

graduationPaths.title: Templates & Resources
graduationPaths.description: Lifetime access to all 41 templates and updates
